compositional_truth_check.p2_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter; soil appears dormant/empty.
compositional_truth_check.p3_black_pot_rabbit: Systemic Loss: The Pothos is absent; only the white rabbit anchor remains.

inventory_reconciliation.notes: The registry indicates four distinct plant specimens. Visual evidence confirms the total absence of all four plants across the monitored pots. The environment is currently devoid of biological growth.

plant_audit.p3: White rabbit anchor present; no Pothos foliage detected.

biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, fragmented material (likely crushed eggshells) in the central black pot area, serving as a non-biological intervention.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: No significant change; pots were already devoid of visible plant life.
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_current: Static state. The environment has remained unchanged throughout the observation window.

visual_health_inference.reasoning: The absence of chlorophyll-bearing tissue or stems in all pots indicates that the specimens are either completely removed, buried, or have suffered total senescence and decomposition.

anomalies.intervention: Introduction of crushed eggshells in the central black pot area.
anomalies.missing_specimens: P1, P2, P3, and P4 are all missing their respective botanical occupants.

vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical biome is currently in a state of total systemic collapse. Despite the presence of the white rabbit anchor in P3, there is no evidence of the Pothos, Mexican Mint, String of Nickels, or Silver Guest. The soil surfaces are barren, and the only notable feature is the presence of white debris (eggshells) in the central pot. No signs of life or active growth are present in the current frame.
